title: Jacob Barandes — Quantum Foundations and Stochastic Dynamics (Collected)
subtitle: 3 papers on no-collapse quantum foundations, Markovian stochastic dynamics, and measurement as instantiation

summary.one_line: Barandes' work on stochastic quantum dynamics provides a formalism for understanding measurement as a physical instantiation of a configuration — directly mapping to the 'measurement = recognition'...
summary.scope: Covers no-collapse quantum foundations, Markovian stochastic quantum dynamics.

sources.label: Jacob Barandes — Collected Papers. 3 PDFs in /root/projects/blog/library/science/authors/barandes/.

body.text: Barandes proposes that quantum collapse is not a separate physical process but a stochastic transition within a Markovian framework — the system naturally transitions to definite states through interactions. This IS the Tantraloka's saṃkoca (contraction): consciousness contracts into a specific configuration through interaction with the environment. Measurement IS the moment of contraction — the stochastic transition from superposition to specificity.
